summary: Implemented sync reflection fallback with from-stashing pattern
reasoning: Per spec section 6.3, added :force_reflect GenServer.call handler following the same from-stashing pattern as :force_observe. When called, checks if observation_tokens < threshold (returns immediately), emits sync_fallback and reflection_started events, spawns Reflector Task with 1 retry max (not 3 like async path) via new run_reflector_with_retry/5 and run_reflector_with_retry_loop/6 functions. Stashes caller's from in sync_from, returns {:noreply, state}. Modified reflector task completion handler to check sync_from and call new handle_sync_reflector_completion/3 function which applies reflection (replaces active_observations, increments generation_count and activation_epoch, checks hard cap, writes snapshot) before replying via GenServer.reply/2. Modified reflector task failure handler to check sync_from and reply with {:error, reason} for sync path. The sync path exists because the system is behind - spending time on retries defeats the purpose, hence 1 retry max. Handle returns {:ok, :below_threshold} if called when observations are below the reflection threshold.

summary: Implemented reflection buffering with epoch-based staleness detection
reasoning: Per spec section 6.2, added buffered reflection support: spawns Reflector Task at 50% of reflection threshold (20,000 tokens), stores result with epoch in buffered_reflection/buffered_reflection_epoch state fields, checks epoch on activation to detect staleness (if epoch changed due to observation activation, discard and re-trigger). Added is_buffering_reflection flag to differentiate buffered vs immediate reflections. Extracted should_activate_reflection?/1 and should_buffer_reflection?/2 helpers to reduce cyclomatic complexity. Buffered reflections activate instantly when full threshold reached and epoch matches.

summary: Fixed sync fallback state update to merge observations before replying
reasoning: Per spec section 6.3, when sync_from is set in observer task completion handler, observations must be merged into active_observations, observed_message_ids updated, and pending_message_tokens decremented BEFORE replying to the caller. Previously, the code replied immediately without updating state, effectively discarding observation data. Extracted sync fallback logic into handle_sync_observer_completion/3 helper to reduce cyclomatic complexity. Now properly merges observations, updates state, writes snapshot, and checks for reflection trigger before replying.

summary: Added epoch staleness check to filter buffered chunks before merging

(10) All handlers are placeholder-safe: job events are caught with catch-all handler that ignores unknown events, preventing crashes if orchestration sends additional event types; (11) Status bar switches seamlessly between session mode (tokens, memory, cost, turn count) and job mode (leads, cost ceiling, elapsed time) based on job_active flag; summary: Implemented SessionPicker Breeze view for session resume
reasoning: Created lib/deft/tui/session_picker.ex to implement session picker view per specs/tui.md section 7. Key decisions: (1) Used Breeze.View with mount/2, render/1, and handle_event/3 following pattern from existing Chat view; (2) mount/2 calls Deft.Session.Store.list/0 to load all sessions - list/0 returns sorted sessions (most-recent-first) with metadata including session_id, working_dir, created_at, last_message_at, message_count, last_user_prompt; (3) State includes sessions list, selected_index (starting at 0), and optional on_select callback function; (4) render/1 displays sessions in a list format using render_session/3 helper - shows session_id (first 8 chars), working_dir (abbreviated with ~ for home, truncated to 30 chars), relative timestamp (Ns/Nm/Nh/Nd ago or YYYY-MM-DD for old sessions), message count, and last user prompt (truncated to 40 chars); (5) Selected session is highlighted with 'bold reverse' style, unselected sessions use normal style; (6) handle_event/3 implements keyboard navigation: Up arrow calls handle_navigation(:up) to decrement selected_index (clamped to 0), Down arrow calls handle_navigation(:down) to increment selected_index (clamped to length-1), Enter calls handle_select/0 to invoke optional on_select callback and send {:session_selected, session_id} message before stopping view, q/Ctrl+C/Ctrl+D stop the view without resuming; (7) Display handles empty state gracefully - shows 'No previous sessions found' message when Store.list/0 returns empty list or error, hides keyboard shortcuts except quit when no sessions; (8) Session line format optimized for readability: '[session_id] working_dir | timestamp | N msgs | last prompt' - session_id abbreviated to 8 chars for compactness, working_dir and prompt truncated with ellipsis if too long; (9) Timestamp formatting uses relative time for recent sessions (seconds/minutes/hours/days ago) and absolute date (YYYY-MM-DD) for sessions older than 7 days - improves UX by making recency immediately visible; (10) Implementation is pure view logic - session resume orchestration (what happens when user selects a session) is left to the caller that mounts this view, enabling flexible integration

summary: Implemented slash command dispatch with parse, route, and error handling

Key decisions: (1) Modified State.start_link/1 to accept optional :messages and :snapshot parameters; (2) Modified init/1 to call restore_from_snapshot/3 when snapshot is provided; (3) Implemented restore_from_snapshot/3 that restores all persisted fields from snapshot (active_observations, observation_tokens, observed_message_ids, generation_count, last_observed_at, activation_epoch, calibration_factor), then recomputes pending_message_tokens from messages not in observed_message_ids using Tokens.estimate/2; (4) After restoration, calls check_and_spawn_observer/1 and check_and_spawn_reflector/1 to trigger observation/reflection if thresholds already exceeded; (5) Added load_latest_snapshot/1 public API that reads from separate _om.jsonl file, parses all lines, returns latest entry (or nil if file doesn't exist); (6) Updated OM.Supervisor to accept and pass :messages and :snapshot to State; (7) Updated Session.Worker to accept :om_snapshot and pass to OM.Supervisor; (8) Updated Session.Supervisor.start_session/1 to accept :om_snapshot option; (9) Updated Store.resume/1 to load OM snapshot from separate _om.jsonl file via State.load_latest_snapshot/1 and return as :om_snapshot (replacing old :om_state from main JSONL); (10) Added comprehensive test suite in state_resume_test.exs covering snapshot restoration, pending token recomputation, fresh start, and snapshot file loading. The separate _om.jsonl file approach (from decision #9) avoids JSONL write interleaving between main session log and OM snapshots.

summary: Emit OM events from State module rather than Observer/Reflector functions
reasoning: Observer.run and Reflector.run are pure functions spawned as Tasks without session_id context. Broadcasting requires session_id for Registry dispatch. Emitting events from State module before/after Task spawning provides cleaner separation and keeps Observer/Reflector as pure extraction/compression functions.

summary: Made parse_sections/1 public in Observer.Parse
reasoning: Hard cap implementation needs to parse observation sections. Rather than duplicate the logic, made the existing private function public for reuse.

summary: Implemented circuit breaker for OM cycle failures
reasoning: Added circuit breaker functionality to Deft.OM.State per spec section 6.3. Key decisions: (1) Added three new state fields: consecutive_failures (integer, tracks cycle failures), circuit_open (boolean, degraded mode flag), circuit_opened_at (DateTime, for cooldown tracking). (2) Implemented record_cycle_success/1 helper that resets consecutive_failures to 0 on successful Observer/Reflector completion - called in handle_info for both observer_ref and reflector_ref success cases. (3) Implemented record_cycle_failure/2 helper that increments consecutive_failures, emits {:om, :cycle_failed, %{type: type, reason: reason}} via broadcast_event/2, checks if circuit should open (>= 3 failures), calls open_circuit/1 if threshold reached - called in handle_info for both observer_ref and reflector_ref :DOWN cases. (4) Added open_circuit/1 helper that sets circuit_open: true, circuit_opened_at: DateTime.utc_now(), logs warning, and emits {:om, :circuit_open} event via broadcast_event/2. (5) Implemented can_attempt_cycle?/1 guard that checks circuit_open flag and calculates cooldown (5 minutes = 300 seconds) - returns true if circuit closed OR if circuit open but cooldown expired. (6) Implemented reset_circuit/1 helper that clears circuit_open, circuit_opened_at, and consecutive_failures - called after successful cooldown in spawn_observer_task/2 and spawn_reflector_task/1. (7) Added broadcast_event/2 helper using Registry.dispatch(Deft.Registry, {:session, session_id}, ...) pattern matching agent.ex - events sent as {:om_event, event} for TUI consumption. (8) Modified spawn_observer_task/2 and spawn_reflector_task/1 to check can_attempt_cycle?/1 before spawning - if circuit open, log debug message and return state unchanged. (9) Modified Observer success handler to check if result.observations is empty (indicates retry failure) and record as failure - prevents treating failed retries as success. Circuit breaker prevents runaway LLM costs during persistent failures and allows automatic recovery after cooldown or explicit /compact command (future work).

summary: Implemented sync fallback for force_observe with 1 retry max
reasoning: Added handle_call for :force_observe that implements the from-stashing pattern per spec section 6.3. Key decisions: (1) On :force_observe call, stash the caller's from in sync_from field, spawn Observer Task with retry logic, return {:noreply, state} to avoid blocking. (2) Added run_observer_with_retry/5 and run_observer_with_retry_loop/6 functions that wrap Observer.run with retry logic - retries up to max_retries times (1 for sync path) with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s...). Empty observations indicates failure. (3) Modified handle_info for Observer Task completion ({ref, result}) to check if sync_from is set - if so, reply via GenServer.reply(sync_from, {:ok, result}) and clear sync_from, otherwise continue with normal async buffering path. (4) Modified handle_info for Observer Task DOWN ({:DOWN, ref, ...}) to check if sync_from is set - if so, reply via GenServer.reply(sync_from, {:error, reason}) and clear sync_from, otherwise continue with normal async error handling. (5) Added early return in force_observe if no unobserved messages exist. The 60s timeout is enforced by the caller (Agent would call GenServer.call(State, :force_observe, 60_000)). This prevents deadlock while allowing State to remain responsive to other calls during the sync observation.

summary: Implemented Observer/Reflector serialization for concurrent coordination
reasoning: Added serialization logic to ensure Observer and Reflector tasks do not run concurrently per spec section 6.4. Key decisions: (1) In check_and_spawn_observer/1, added check for not state.is_reflecting before activating buffered chunks - if Reflector is running, Observer results remain buffered until reflection completes. (2) After Observer completes (handle_info for observer_ref), added call to check_and_spawn_reflector/1 to trigger reflection now that is_observing is false. (3) After Reflector completes (handle_info for reflector_ref), added check for buffered chunks - if chunks exist and pending_message_tokens >= threshold, activate them now that is_reflecting is false. (4) Refactored check_and_spawn_observer/1 to reduce cyclomatic complexity from 10 to acceptable level by extracting two helpers: maybe_activate_buffered_chunks/1 (checks threshold + chunks + not reflecting, calls activate_buffered_chunks if true) and maybe_spawn_observer_for_buffering/3 (handles observer spawning logic with buffer intervals). (5) activation_epoch increments were already in place (activate_buffered_chunks line 393, Reflector completion line 242) per spec requirement. (6) The serialization rule is bidirectional: check_and_spawn_reflector/1 already checks not state.is_observing before spawning Reflector (line 404), and now check_and_spawn_observer checks not state.is_reflecting before activating chunks. This prevents race conditions where Reflector replaces active_observations while Observer results are computed against stale pre-reflection observations. Both processes can buffer work in parallel, but activation is serialized.

summary: Implemented Deft.Issue.Id.generate/1 for unique hash-based IDs
reasoning: Created Deft.Issue.Id module per spec section 2.1 (ID Generation). Key decisions: (1) Used :crypto.strong_rand_bytes(16) instead of UUID library since no UUID dependency exists in mix.exs - provides cryptographically strong random data. (2) Converted random bytes to lowercase hex string using Base.encode16(case: :lower) for consistent formatting. (3) Implemented recursive collision detection via find_unique_id/3 helper that starts with 4 hex characters and extends to 5, 6, etc. on collision. (4) Used simple linear search (candidate_id in existing_ids) for collision detection - acceptable for small issue tracker scale (tens to hundreds of issues). (5) Created comprehensive test suite with 11 test cases covering ID format validation, uniqueness, collision handling, and edge cases. Module provides ID generation for issue creation - GenServer and persistence will be implemented in subsequent work items.

summary: Defined Deft.Issue struct with JSON encode/decode support
reasoning: Created Deft.Issue struct module per spec section 2 (Issue Schema). Key decisions: (1) Used @derive Jason.Encoder for automatic JSON encoding following existing patterns in session/entry.ex. (2) Implemented encode/1 wrapper that returns {:ok, json} | {:error, reason} tuple for consistent error handling. (3) Implemented decode/1 that uses Jason.decode with keys: :atoms option matching patterns in session/store.ex and other JSONL-handling code. (4) Added from_map/1 helper to convert decoded JSON maps to Issue structs with proper type coercion. (5) Implemented normalize_status/1 and normalize_source/1 private functions to handle both atom and string representations of status (:open | :in_progress | :closed) and source (:user | :agent) fields - this enables robust decoding from JSON where these are serialized as strings. (6) Added timestamp/0 helper function that returns DateTime.utc_now() |> DateTime.to_iso8601() per spec section 2 requirement for consistent ISO 8601 UTC timestamp format across all Issue fields. (7) Used @enforce_keys for all required fields except closed_at and job_id which are explicitly nullable per spec section 2. (8) Included comprehensive moduledoc and function docs with examples for encode/decode/from_map/timestamp functions. Module provides foundational data structure for issue tracking - GenServer, persistence, and CLI commands will be implemented in subsequent work items.

summary: Implemented Reflector prompt with configurable compression levels
reasoning: Created Deft.OM.Reflector.Prompt module per spec section 4. Implemented system/1 function that takes :target_size (default 20k tokens) and :compression_level (0-3) parameters. Key decisions: (1) Created separate compression_level_guidance/1 helper that returns level-specific instructions (Level 0: gentle/judgmental, Level 1: moderate with 1-day retention, Level 2: aggressive dropping all 🟢 items, Level 3: maximum compression keeping only 🔴 and most recent day). (2) Included section ordering constraint with MUST PRESERVE THIS ORDER emphasis (Current State, User Preferences, Files & Architecture, Decisions, Session History) per spec section 4.4. (3) Added per-section token budget guidance per spec section 4.4: Current State ~500, User Preferences ~1k, Files & Architecture ~8k, Decisions ~3k, Session History remainder. (4) Emphasized CORRECTION marker preservation with CRITICAL label and explicit instructions to keep verbatim at all costs per spec section 4.4. (5) Implemented format_number/1 helper to format target_size with thousands separator for readability (20000 → "20,000"). (6) Structured prompt with clear sections: Compression Task (target + level guidance), Section Structure (ordering + budgets), CORRECTION Markers (preservation rules), Compression Strategies (merge/drop/preserve patterns), What NOT to Do (constraints), Output Format (markdown structure). Module provides system prompt generation for Reflector LLM calls - actual Task execution will be implemented in separate work item.

summary: Implemented observation activation with section-aware merge
reasoning: Added activation logic to State.check_and_spawn_observer/1 that triggers when pending_message_tokens >= 30k AND buffered_chunks is non-empty. Key decisions: (1) Activation happens before spawning new observer tasks - this ensures buffered chunks are merged before starting new buffer cycles. (2) Used Deft.OM.Observer.Parse.merge_observations/2 to perform section-aware merge - Current State section is replaced, User Preferences/Decisions/Session History are appended, Files & Architecture uses append-with-dedup. (3) All message_ids from all chunks are collected and added to observed_message_ids. (4) Pending tokens are reduced by the sum of all chunk message_tokens (the raw token count that was compressed). (5) activation_epoch is incremented and snapshot_dirty is set to true. (6) last_observed_at is set to DateTime.utc_now(). (7) Fixed bug in State and Supervisor via_tuple functions - they were using Deft.Registry (duplicate keys for pub/sub) instead of Deft.ProcessRegistry (unique keys for :via tuples), causing "via is not supported for duplicate registries" error. Created comprehensive test suite with 6 test cases covering activation logic, section-aware merge behavior, and edge cases (no activation when pending < threshold, no activation when chunks empty).

summary: Implemented Observer Task execution with actual LLM calls
reasoning: Replaced placeholder Observer Task with full implementation. Key decisions: (1) Created Deft.OM.Observer module with run/4 function that takes Config, messages, existing_observations, and calibration_factor; formats messages using Prompt.format_messages/1, truncates observations to 8k budget using Prompt.truncate_observations/2, builds system+user messages, calls LLM via provider.stream/3, collects response with collect_stream_text/2 helper (120s timeout), parses using Parse.parse_output/1, returns map with observations/message_ids/message_tokens/current_task fields. (2) Used same provider as main agent for Observer since config doesn't have separate om.observer_provider field yet (spec section 8 mentions it but current Config implementation only has om_observer_model) - reasonable default and can be extended later without breaking changes. (3) Modified State struct to include config and messages fields (not just tokens) since Observer needs full message content; updated @enforce_keys and defstruct accordingly. (4) Modified State.messages_added/2 to store full Message structs and calculate tokens using Tokens.estimate/2 on extracted message text (handles all content block types: Text, ToolUse, ToolResult, Thinking, Image). (5) Updated spawn_observer_task/2 to filter unobserved messages (id not in observed_message_ids) and call Observer.run/4 with proper parameters. (6) Threaded config through supervisor chain: Session.Worker passes config to OM.Supervisor, OM.Supervisor passes config to State, State uses config when spawning Observer tasks. (7) Used Task.Supervisor.async_nolink for Observer tasks per spec section 1 (crash isolation). (8) Observer returns empty observations on any failure (parse error, LLM error, provider resolution error) but still tracks message_ids and message_tokens so State knows which messages were attempted. (9) Aliased nested modules (Deft.OM.Observer.{Parse, Prompt}) and Message content block types to satisfy Credo strict mode - no unused aliases.

summary: Implemented Deft.OM.Observer.Parse module with XML parsing and section-aware merge
reasoning: Created Observer.Parse module per spec sections 3.5 and 3.6. Key decisions: (1) Implemented parse_output/1 to extract <observations> and <current-task> from XML using regex patterns; validates section headers against allowed set (Current State, User Preferences, Files & Architecture, Decisions, Session History); falls back to raw bullet-list extraction when XML tags missing but section headers present; returns error when no structure found. (2) Implemented merge_observations/2 with section-aware merge strategies: Current State uses replace strategy (regenerated each cycle), User Preferences/Decisions/Session History use append strategy, Files & Architecture uses append-with-dedup strategy keyed by filepath (extracts filepaths from 'Read <path>' and 'Modified <path>' patterns, updates existing entries with same path), unknown sections ignored per spec. (3) Implemented parse_sections/1 helper to parse observations into map of section_name => content by scanning for '## ' headers. (4) Implemented extract_filepath/1 helper to extract filepaths from file observation lines using regex matching 'Read' and 'Modified' patterns. Module maintains canonical section ordering (Current State, User Preferences, Files & Architecture, Decisions, Session History) in merged output. Created comprehensive test suite with 16 test cases covering all merge strategies and edge cases.

summary: Implemented Deft.OM.Observer.Prompt module with system prompt and input formatting
reasoning: Created Observer.Prompt module per spec sections 3.3, 3.4, and 3.5. Key decisions: (1) Implemented system/0 function with comprehensive extraction rules covering 🔴 high-priority (user facts, preferences), 🟡 medium-priority (files, errors, commands, architecture, deps, git state, TODOs), and 🟢 low-priority items; included anti-hallucination rules (only extract stated facts, use 'Likely:' prefix for uncertain observations, don't record hypotheticals/explorations); specified sectioned output format (Current State, User Preferences, Files & Architecture, Decisions, Session History) with XML wrapping (<observations> and <current-task> tags). (2) Implemented format_messages/1 to convert Deft.Message structs to readable text format with timestamps (HH:MM), role labels, tool call/result annotations ([Tool Call: name], [Tool Result: name]), and image placeholders; skips thinking blocks as they're internal reasoning; truncates very long tool results to ~500 chars for readability. (3) Implemented truncate_observations/2 following spec's 8k budget strategy: takes last 5k tokens (tail), scans remainder for 🔴 high-priority lines filling to 3k budget, prepends '[N observations truncated]' marker if content removed. All functions use Deft.OM.Tokens.estimate/2 with 4.0 calibration factor for consistent token counting. Module provides all input preparation for Observer LLM calls - actual LLM invocation will be implemented in separate Observer.run/2 work item.

summary: Implemented Deft.Store GenServer with ETS+DETS dual registry pattern
reasoning: Implemented Deft.Store GenServer per spec section 2. Key decisions: (1) Added Deft.ProcessRegistry (unique keys) to Application supervisor for :via tuple registration, separate from existing Deft.Registry (duplicate keys for pub/sub events). (2) Used :public ETS table instead of :protected because async load task (separate process) needs write access; access control enforced at GenServer API level via write/4 validation, not ETS level. (3) Separated do_shutdown (flush+close) from do_cleanup (shutdown+delete) so terminate doesn't delete DETS file on normal shutdown - preserves data for restart while cleanup explicitly destroys everything. (4) Registry-based access control for site log uses Registry.lookup(Deft.ProcessRegistry, owner_name) to resolve to PID and compare with caller, survives Foreman process restarts because restarted process re-registers under same name. (5) Async load task spawned with Task.async (linked to GenServer), handles both success ({ref, :loaded}) and failure ({:DOWN, ref, ...}) messages, brutal_kill on cleanup if still running. (6) Cache: lazy flush on buffer size (50 entries) OR timer (5 seconds), no :dets.sync/1 (loss acceptable). Site log: immediate write with :dets.sync/1 on every write (low volume, durability critical). (7) DETS corruption recovery: on :dets.open_file/2 error, delete corrupted file and create new empty file; log warning for site log corruption, silent for cache corruption (ephemeral data).

summary: Implemented Deft.OM.State GenServer with stub Observer Task
reasoning: Implemented the State GenServer per spec sections 1 and 2. Key decisions: (1) Converted State from struct-only module to full GenServer with init/1, handle_call, handle_cast, and handle_info callbacks. (2) Implemented get_context/1 API returning {active_observations, observed_message_ids} tuple as specified in work item. (3) Implemented messages_added/2 API that updates pending_message_tokens and triggers Observer Task spawning at buffer intervals (every 20% of 30k threshold = 6k tokens). (4) Implemented coalescing logic using is_observing and needs_rebuffer flags - if already observing when threshold crossed, sets rebuffer flag instead of spawning duplicate task; when current task completes, checks flag and spawns new task if needed. (5) Added observer_ref and last_buffer_threshold fields to state struct to track in-flight tasks and prevent duplicate spawns. (6) Created stub Observer Task (returns empty observations) since Observer module implementation is a separate work item - infrastructure is complete and will be replaced when Observer.run/2 is implemented. (7) Used Task.Supervisor.async_nolink to spawn Observer tasks for crash isolation per spec section 1. (8) Handled both successful task completion ({ref, result}) and task failure ({:DOWN, ref, ...}) in handle_info with proper cleanup of is_observing flag and observer_ref.

summary: Implemented fixture validation tooling to detect stale fixtures
reasoning: Created Deft.Eval.FixtureValidator module and mix eval.validate_fixtures task per spec sections 1.3 and 5. Key decisions: (1) Placed module in lib/deft/eval/ rather than test/eval/support/ so it compiles and is available for mix tasks - validation is a development tool, not test-specific code. (2) Defined @spec_mapping to map fixture directory names to spec names (observer->observational-memory, foreman->orchestration, etc.) for maintainability. (3) Supported both direct spec .md files and subdirectory README.md formats for spec discovery. (4) Excluded hidden fixture files (starting with .) from validation - these are examples/documentation. (5) Extracted version parsing into separate extract_version_from_file/1 function to reduce nesting depth and satisfy Credo strict mode. (6) Added make test.eval.validate_fixtures target for convenient execution. Validation reports total/valid/stale/invalid counts with detailed lists of problematic fixtures showing current vs expected versions.

summary: Created holdout fixture set infrastructure
reasoning: Implemented the holdout fixture set per spec section 1.4. Key decisions: (1) Created fixtures directory structure with holdout subdirectory containing 5 categories (coding_conversations, codebase_snapshots, observation_sets, tool_results, issue_transcripts) mirroring the main fixtures structure. (2) Updated Makefile to add test.eval.holdout target (runs with --only eval --only holdout) and modified test.eval target to exclude holdout tests (--only eval --exclude holdout). (6) Results directory already in .gitignore per spec section 2.1. (7) Created support/ directory for future eval helpers. The infrastructure is now in place for developers to add holdout fixtures following the documented guidelines.

summary: Implemented Deft.OM.Supervisor with rest_for_one strategy
reasoning: Implemented the OM supervisor per spec section 1. Key decisions: (1) TaskSupervisor starts first, State second - with rest_for_one strategy, if TaskSupervisor crashes, State also restarts to clear stale is_observing/is_reflecting flags. (2) Both TaskSupervisor and State are registered via Registry with session-scoped names using {:via, Registry, {Deft.Registry, {:om_task_supervisor, session_id}}} pattern for discoverability. (3) Exposed task_supervisor_name/1 as public API so State GenServer (next work item) can reference the TaskSupervisor when spawning Tasks. (4) Passed session_id to State child spec for its own initialization.

summary: Added epoch field to BufferedChunk struct
reasoning: Work item explicitly mentioned epoch field for BufferedChunk. Spec section 6.1 describes epoch-based staleness detection for late-arriving chunks. Added to struct even though not listed in section 2 table.

summary: Implemented comprehensive starvation protection tests using injectable time source
reasoning: Added three test cases to verify starvation protection behavior: (1) 'promotes low-priority requests after 10 seconds' - verifies that a lead request enqueued before a foreman request gets promoted to highest priority after waiting 10s, and is processed first due to earlier enqueue time. (2) 'promotes multiple starved requests in enqueue order' - verifies that multiple requests all waiting >10s are promoted while maintaining their original enqueue order (FIFO among promoted requests). (3) 'does not promote requests that haven't waited long enough' - verifies that requests waiting <10s are not promoted prematurely. Used :atomics for thread-safe time manipulation in tests, allowing precise control over simulated time without actual delays. Tests verify the existing starvation protection implementation works correctly by injecting a custom time source function that reads from the atomics counter.

summary: Implemented priority queue with starvation protection for RateLimiter
reasoning: Added priority queue to RateLimiter GenServer using :gb_trees with keys {-priority, queue_id} for proper ordering. Key decisions: (1) Priority hierarchy: Foreman (3) > Runner (2) > Lead (1) to avoid priority inversion - Runners are spawned by Leads and Leads block on them, so prioritizing Leads over Runners would starve Leads' active work. (2) Used negative priority in queue key to ensure higher numeric priorities dequeue first with :gb_trees.take_smallest. (3) queue_id monotonic counter ensures FIFO within same priority level. (4) Starvation protection via periodic :check_queue timer (1s interval) that scans queue for requests older than 10s and promotes them to highest priority. (5) Queue processing is recursive - after granting permission to one request, immediately tries to process next until buckets exhausted. (6) Reconcile triggers queue processing to unblock waiting requests when capacity becomes available. (7) Injectable time_source in state for testability. (8) Updated API to accept caller_type (:foreman/:runner/:lead) parameter with backward-compatible default of :lead.

summary: Implemented dual token-bucket GenServer with RPM and TPM buckets
reasoning: Created Deft.Job.RateLimiter GenServer following spec section 1. Key decisions: (1) Used hardcoded Anthropic Tier 1 limits (50 RPM, 40k TPM) as defaults since providers.md doesn't define rate limits yet - added TODO to move to configuration when spec is updated. (2) Implemented blocking retry with Process.sleep(100) when buckets insufficient - noted this is temporary until priority queue work item is implemented. (3) Token estimation uses chars/4 heuristic as specified, with support for simple string content, content arrays, and tool_use blocks via JSON encoding. (4) Credit-back after reconciliation is capped at bucket capacity to prevent over-crediting that would allow bursts above configured TPM. (5) Bucket refill uses monotonic time and calculates elapsed seconds to add tokens proportionally - buckets always refill before checking/deducting capacity.

summary: Placed judge calibration module in lib/deft/eval/ rather than test/eval/support/
reasoning: Judge calibration functionality needs to be compiled and available at runtime, not just during test execution. The module provides APIs for loading calibration examples, running calibration, calculating precision/recall metrics, and saving results. Test support code in test/ is not compiled into the application, but calibration can be run as a standalone task via mix tasks or interactive sessions, not just during ExUnit tests. Documentation and example datasets remain in test/eval/support/judge_calibration/ as they are reference materials, not executable code.

summary: Verified Tribunal v1.3.5 provides all required assertions and evaluation capabilities
reasoning: Investigated Tribunal package on hex.pm and GitHub. Confirmed v1.3.5 (released 2026-03-05) provides all required deterministic assertions (contains, regex, is_json, max_tokens), LLM-as-judge assertions (faithful, hallucination, pii via req_llm dependency), and evaluation mode (mix tribunal.eval with threshold support). No gaps identified. Documented findings in docs/spikes/tribunal_verification.md including recommendations to use ~> 1.3 version constraint, include req_llm for LLM-as-judge, and leverage ExUnit integration pattern. Ready for eval infrastructure implementation.

summary: Implemented eval diffing command to compare two runs
reasoning: Created mix eval.compare task that loads two eval run JSONL files and compares them. Key decisions: (1) Read JSONL files directly instead of using ResultStore.load() to handle multiple category results per run file, since the spec indicates one file per run_id but potentially multiple categories. (2) Refactored print functions into smaller helpers to reduce nesting depth for Credo compliance. (3) Added ANSI color coding (green for improvements, red for regressions) and soft floor violation warnings for better visual feedback. (4) Show up to 3 failure examples per category for side-by-side comparison without overwhelming output.

summary: Implemented baselines with history tracking and update logic
reasoning: Created Deft.Eval.Baselines module to manage baseline tracking per spec section 2.2. Baselines store baseline rate, soft_floor (baseline - 10pp), and history array per category. Update logic ensures baselines only go up - new results are added to history, but baseline only increases if new rate exceeds current baseline. Soft floor is recalculated on each baseline update. Empty baselines.json created at test/eval/ with structure ready for first eval runs. All 17 tests pass including load/save, baseline-only-increases behavior, soft floor checking, and full integration workflow.

summary: Implemented project directory layout with Deft.Project module
reasoning: Created Deft.Project module to handle project-scoped directory creation under ~/.deft/projects/<path-encoded-repo>/ with sessions/, cache/, and jobs/ subdirectories. Path encoding replaces / with - and strips leading -. Git worktree detection via 'git rev-parse --show-toplevel' ensures monorepos share a single project directory. Symlinks resolved via Path.expand/1 before encoding. All tests pass including comprehensive coverage of encoding, git root resolution, and directory creation.

summary: Implemented eval result storage with automatic cleanup
reasoning: Created Deft.Eval.ResultStore module for storing per-run results as JSONL with all required fields (run_id, commit, timestamp, model, category, pass_rate, iterations, cost_usd, failures). Implemented automatic cleanup to keep only last 30 runs. Created mix eval.export task for archiving results. Added test/eval/results/ to .gitignore.

summary: Major evals spec expansion based on 3-reviewer expert panel
reasoning: Deployed ML/AI eval expert, testing strategy expert, and product/UX eval expert in parallel. Key outcomes: increased iterations from 10 to 20-30, added holdout set requirement, added threshold tuning grid search methodology, moved format assertions from statistical to hard, added end-to-end task battery, added overnight loop safety evals, added bootstrapping strategy for pre-agent evals.

summary: Threshold tuning via grid search, not guessing
reasoning: Per-tool configurable thresholds with defaults determined by grid search over [2k,4k,8k,12k,16k,24k] tokens against 20 realistic tasks. Measure task completion vs context consumption, find the knee. Different tools have different information densities.

summary: Verification circuit breaker is highest-priority eval
reasoning: If the Foreman marks broken work as done, every other eval is suspect. This is the safety net for the entire pipeline. False positives here are the most expensive failure mode. Must be built before end-to-end evals.

summary: Rejected finding: turn counter off-by-one already fixed
reasoning: Agent reported turn_count not incremented on initial call, but specd_history.md shows this was already fixed — check changed from > to >= at line 1140, making total calls equal max_turns exactly.

summary: Rejected finding: async compaction is intentional design
reasoning: Agent reported async compaction doesn't protect triggering call. But async compaction was itself a fix (specd_history.md line 8) to avoid blocking gen_statem. The 70% threshold leaves 30% headroom — the triggering call won't exceed the window.

summary: Marked harness v0.1 as Implemented — no findings
reasoning: Audit found 0 confirmed findings. Both agent-reported issues were either already fixed (turn counter) or intentional design (async compaction). All spec requirements are met.

summary: ETS+DETS over human-readable files for cache/diary storage
reasoning: Cache and diary are runtime state, not user-facing data. Nobody needs to cat these files. Binary DETS is fine — tools interact through the GenServer API. ETS gives concurrent reads without process bottleneck. DETS handles crash recovery persistence.

summary: Replace Site Log with OTP messages + diary GenServer
reasoning: Site Log was fighting OTP — serializing to JSONL when message passing is the native coordination mechanism. The BEAM provides FIFO ordering, prevents collisions. Diary GenServer replaces data function, direct process messages replace coordination function.

summary: Diary is Foreman-write only, programmatic promotion via pattern matching
reasoning: Foreman has the birds-eye view. Programmatic rules (auto-promote contracts, decisions, corrections; never promote status/blockers) are cheap and predictable. No LLM call needed. Foreman can still write arbitrary entries via its own judgment for edge cases.

summary: Commands are just markdown prompts, skills are structured YAML
reasoning: Two levels of complexity: commands are trivially easy to create (write a markdown file, drop it in directory). Skills are richer (YAML manifest + definition, progressive loading). Both invoked via slash command.

reasoning: Mirrors config cascade. Users override built-in behavior without forking. Projects specialize without affecting global. Global skills in ~/.deft/skills/, project skills in .deft/skills/.

summary: Moved retry counter reset from :calling → :streaming to successful stream completion
reasoning: The retry counters (retry_count and retry_delay) were being reset at three transition points from :calling to :streaming (lines 285-286, 304-305, 323-324). This broke the retry logic: when a stream partially succeeded then errored, the retry would transition back to :calling, and on the next stream start would reset the counters to 0, allowing unlimited retries instead of the spec's 'up to 3 times' limit. Fixed by removing resets from those transitions and adding reset to handle_stream_done (when stream successfully completes). Retry counters are now only reset on: (1) successful stream completion, (2) new user prompt, (3) new provider call after tool execution, (4) max retries exceeded — all appropriate locations.

summary: Confirmed finding: retry counter reset on streaming transition allows infinite retries
reasoning: Agent.ex:284-286 resets retry_count to 0 and retry_delay to 1000 on every :calling → :streaming transition. If a stream partially succeeds then errors, handle_stream_error increments retry_count from 0, transitions back to :calling, and the next successful stream start resets it again. This bypasses the spec's 'up to 3 times' limit. Verified against actual code — the reset at line 285 is unconditional.

Fixed by storing both task.ref and task.pid in task_info maps (for tool tasks) and data.compaction_task_pid (for compaction task), then passing the PID to terminate_child. This ensures abort properly kills in-flight tool and compaction tasks instead of orphaning them. Agent research claimed 4 findings; rejected 3 (test isolation issue, om_enabled default correct for now, String.to_atom is anti-pattern but not functional breakage). Confirmed 1.

summary: Added Process.monitor/1 for stream PID with nil-safe guarding
